title: Wearable health monitors and methods of monitoring health
abstract: Wearable technologies, such as wearable health monitors, and methods of use are provided. In some embodiments, the wearable technology can be worn at the wrist of an individual and can use an accelerometer, pulse oximeter, and electrocardiogram to measure heart rate, oxygen saturation, blood pressure, pulse wave velocity, and activity. This information can then be provided to the individual. The individual can alter their behaviors and relationships with their own health by using features such as notifications and auto-tagging to better understand their own stress, diet, sleep, and exercise levels over various time periods and subsequently make appropriate behavioral changes.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A wearable electronic apparatus for monitoring a user's cardiovascular health, the apparatus comprising: an attachment structure for attaching the wearable apparatus to a predefined body location of the user; and an electronics module coupled to the attachment structure, the electronics module comprising: a plurality of sensors comprising at least an electrocardiogram (ECG) sensor for measuring data including at least one of the heart rate, pulse wave velocity (PWV), pulse transit time (PTT), and a pulse oximeter sensor with photoplethysmography (PPG) for measuring oxygen saturation of the user; a processor module coupled to the plurality of sensors for collecting data therefrom and for calculating blood pressure using a machine learning method based at least one collected data; and an output module coupled to the processor module outputting at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data. 2. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the pulse oximeter sensor comprises a Pulse LED and a light sensor. 3. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 2, wherein the Pulse LED is configured to emit a first light at a first wavelength and a second light 

 at a second wavelength, and wherein the light sensor is configured to receive at least a portion of the first light reflected from a skin of the user and at least a portion of the second light reflected from a skin of the user. 4. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 3, wherein the first wavelength is about 660nm and the second wavelength is about 940nm. 5. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the electronics module further comprises an accelerometer for collecting the movement data of the user. 6. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the processor module calculates blood pressure using the machine learning method based on collected data and the user's historical health data. 7. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the machine learning method is a polynomial regression analysis method, a neural network, a Bayesian network, a decision tree, an adaptive logic network, or a support vector machines. 8. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 7 wherein the attachment structure is a band. 9. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 8 wherein the band is configured to position the wearable electronic apparatus on a wrist of the user. 10. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 8 or 9 wherein the band is configured to position the electronics module in proximity with a blood vessel of the user. 1 1 . The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 8 or 9 wherein the blood vessel is a distal radial artery. 12. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the output module is a display on the wearable electronic apparatus. 13. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 12, wherein the display is a multi-color LED. 14. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 12 or 13, wherein the display is configured to notify the user if the plurality of sensors are ready to obtain readings, if the wearable electronic apparatus is converting the readings to output data, and if the wearable electronic apparatus has successfully completed collection of data from the sensors. 15. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the output module comprises a communication component for transmitting at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data to a device external to the wearable electronic apparatus. 16. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the communication component is a wireless communication module. 17. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 16, wherein the wireless communication module is a Bluetooth module. 18. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the attachment structure further comprises a connection for receiving a smartwatch. 19. The wearable electronic apparatus of claim 18, wherein the connection is a pin connection. 20. The wearable electronic apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the electronics module further comprises at least one circuit board, a battery and a battery charging circuit. 21 . A method for monitoring a user's cardiovascular health, the method comprising: collecting data of the user using at least one sensor, said data comprising at least one of the heart rate, pulse wave velocity (PWV), pulse transit time (PTT), and oxygen saturation; calculating blood pressure of the user using a machine learning method based at least one collected data; and outputting at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data. 22. The method of claim 21 wherein the collecting data step comprises: collecting at least one of the heart rate, pulse wave velocity (PWV), pulse transit time (PTT) of the user using at least an electrocardiogram (ECG) sensor; and collecting oxygen saturation of the user using a pulse oximeter sensor with photoplethysmography (PPG). 23. The method of claim 22 wherein collecting oxygen saturation of the user using a pulse oximeter sensor comprises: emitting a first light at a first wavelength and a second light at a second wavelength; 

 receiving at least a portion of the first light reflected from a skin of the user and at least a portion of the second light reflected from a skin of the user; determining a first reading corresponding to the amount of the first light being absorbed by the blood under the skin and a second reading corresponding to the amount of the second light being absorbed by the skin; and calculating the oxygen saturation of the user using the ratio between the first and second readings. 24. The method of claim 23 wherein the first wavelength is about 660nm and the second wavelength is about 940nm. 25. The method of any one of claims claim 21 to 24 further comprising: collecting the movement data of the user using an accelerometer. 26. The method of any one of claims 21 to 25 wherein the calculating blood pressure step comprises: calculating blood pressure using the machine learning method based on collected data and the user's historical health data. 27. The method of any one of claims 21 to 26 wherein the machine learning method is a polynomial regression analysis method, a neural network, a 

 Bayesian network, a decision tree, an adaptive logic network, or a support vector machines. 28. The method of any one of claims 21 to 27 further comprising: attaching the at least one sensor on a wrist of the user. 29. The method of claim 28 wherein the attaching step comprises: attaching the at least one sensor on the wrist of the user using a band. 30. The method of claim 28 or 29 wherein the attaching step comprises: attaching the at least one sensor on the wrist of the user in proximity with a blood vessel of the user. 31 . The method of claim 30 wherein the blood vessel is a distal radial artery. 32. The method of any one of claims 21 to 31 wherein the outputting step comprises: displaying at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data. 33. The method of claim 32 wherein the displaying step comprises: displaying at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data using a multi-color LED. 34. The method of claim 32 or 33 wherein the displaying step comprises: notifying the user if the at least one sensor is ready to obtain readings, if the readings are being converted to output data, and if collection of data from the sensors has been successfully completed. 35. The method of any one of claims 21 to 30 wherein the outputting step comprises: transmitting at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data to a remote device. 36. The method of claim 35 wherein the transmitting step comprises: wirelessly transmitting at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data to the remote device. 37. The method of claim 36 wherein the transmitting step comprises: transmitting at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data to the remote device using Bluetooth. 38. The method of any one of claims 21 to 37 further comprising: attaching the at least one sensor about a smartwatch. 39. The method of claim 38 wherein attaching the at least one sensor about the smartwatch comprises: attaching the at least one sensor about the smartwatch using a pin connection. 40. A system for monitoring a user's cardiovascular health, the system comprising: an attachment structure for attaching the wearable apparatus to a predefined body location of the user; an electronics module coupled to the attachment structure; and a device external to the attachment structure; wherein the electronics module comprises: a plurality of sensors comprising at least an electrocardiogram (ECG) sensor for measuring data including at least one of the heart rate, pulse wave 

 velocity (PWV), pulse transit time (PTT), and a pulse oximeter sensor with photoplethysmography (PPG) for measuring oxygen saturation of the user; a processor module coupled to the plurality of sensors for collecting data therefrom and; and an output module coupled to the processor module outputting at least one of collected data to the external device; and wherein the external device calculates blood pressure using a machine learning method based at least one data received from the output module. 41 . The system of claim 40, wherein the pulse oximeter sensor comprises a Pulse LED and a light sensor. 42. The system claim 41 , wherein the Pulse LED is configured to emit a first light at a first wavelength and a second light at a second wavelength, and wherein the light sensor is configured to receive at least a portion of the first light reflected from a skin of the user and at least a portion of the second light reflected from a skin of the user. 43. The system of claim 42, wherein the first wavelength is about 660nm and the second wavelength is about 940nm. 44. The system of any one of claims 40 to 43 wherein the electronics module further comprises an accelerometer for collecting the movement data of the user. 45. The system of any one of claims 40 to 44, wherein the processor module calculates blood pressure using the machine learning method based on collected data and the user's historical health data. 46. The system of any one of claims 40 to 45, wherein the machine learning method is a polynomial regression analysis method, a neural network, a Bayesian network, a decision tree, an adaptive logic network, or a support vector machines. 47. The system of any one of claims 40 to 46 wherein the attachment structure is a band. 48. The system of claim 47 wherein the band is configured to position the wearable electronic apparatus on a wrist of the user. 49. The system of claim 47 or 48 wherein the band is configured to position the electronics module in proximity with a blood vessel of the user. 50. The system of claim 49 wherein the blood vessel is a distal radial artery. 51 . The system of any one of claims 40 to 50, wherein the output module is a display on the wearable electronic apparatus. 52. The system of claim 51 , wherein the display is a multi-color LED. 53. The system of claim 51 or 52, wherein the display is configured to notify the user if the plurality of sensors are ready to obtain readings, if the wearable electronic apparatus is converting the readings to output data, and if the wearable electronic apparatus has successfully completed collection of data from the sensors. 54. The system of any one of claims 40 to 53, wherein the output module comprises a communication component for transmitting at least one of the calculated blood pressure and the collected data to a device external to the wearable electronic apparatus. 55. The system of any one of claims 40 to 54, wherein the communication component is a wireless communication module. 56. The system of claim 55, wherein the wireless communication module is a Bluetooth module. 57. The system of any one of claims 40 to 56, wherein the attachment structure further comprises a connection for receiving a smartwatch. 58. The system of claim 57, wherein the connection is a pin connection. 59. The system of any one of claims 40 to 58, wherein the electronics module further comprises at least one circuit board, a battery and a battery charging circuit.
